Fine white dust hangs in the air, illuminated by the low morning light cutting through the bakery window
The rough, honest topography of the counter grain beneath my palms, a perimeter that doesn't shift
Fine, pale rye dust in the creases of my knuckles, marking the honest work of the morning
Fine white dust dancing in a shaft of morning light and the lingering pressure on my right shoulder
The dry, chalky skin of flour on my forearms marks where I’ve been and what I’ve done
